author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It may be attributed to David himself, not the Mediator, the Man Christ Jesus, but the whole Church now perfectly established in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Judge me, O Lord, for I have walked in my innocence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Judge me, O Lord, for, after the mercy which Thou first showedst me, I have some desert of my innocence, the way whereof I have kept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And trusting in the Lord I shall not be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet not even so trusting in myself, but in the Lord, I shall abide in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Prove me, O Lord, and try me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest, however, any of my secret sins should be hid from me, prove me, O Lord, and try me, making me known, not to Thee from whom nothing is hid, but to myself, and to men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Burn my reins and my heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Apply a remedial purgation, as it were fire, to my pleasures and thoughts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy mercy is before mine eyes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, that I be not consumed by that fire, not my merits, but Thy mercy, whereby Thou hast brought me on to such a life, is before my eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have been pleasing in Thy truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since my own falsehood hath been displeasing to me, but Thy truth pleasing, I have myself been pleasing also with it and in it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have not sat with the council of vanity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not chosen to give my heart to them who endeavour to provide, what is impossible, how they may be blessed in the enjoyment of things transitory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will not enter in with them that work wickedly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since this is the very cause of all wickedness, therefore I will not have my conscience hid, with them that work wickedly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have hated the congregation of evil doers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to arrive at this council of vanity, congregations of evil doers are formed, which I have hated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will not sit with the ungodly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, therefore, with such a council, with the ungodly, I will not sit, that is, I will not place my consent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will not sit with the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will wash mine hands amid the innocent” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will make clean my works among the innocent: among the innocent will I wash mine hands, with which I shall embrace Thy glorious gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will compass Thy altar, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may hear the voice of Thy praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I may learn how to praise Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And that I may declare all Thy wondrous works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And after I have learnt, I may set forth all Thy wondrous works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, I have loved the beauty of Thy house:” of Thy Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the place of the habitation of Thy glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8): where Thou dwellest, and art glorified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Destroy not my soul with the ungodly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Destroy not then, together with them that hate Thee, my soul, which hath loved the beauty of Thy house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my life with the men of blood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And with them that hate their neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thy house is beautified with the two commandments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In whose hands is wickedness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Destroy me not then with the ungodly and the men of blood, whose works are wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their right hand is full of gifts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that which was given them to obtain eternal salvation, they have converted into the receiving this world’s gifts, “supposing that godliness is a trade.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I have walked in mine innocence: deliver me, and have mercy on me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let so great a price of my Lord’s Blood avail for my complete deliverance: and in the dangers of this life let not Thy mercy leave me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My foot hath stood in uprightness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My Love hath not withdrawn from Thy righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the Churches I will bless Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will not hide Thy blessing, O Lord, from those whom Thou hast called; for next to the love of Thee I join the love of my neighbour.
